vision_report.inventory_reconciliation: The registry identifies P2 (Mexican Mint) as present, but visual evidence confirms a systemic loss of the specimen. The soil in the shared P2/P4 black pot shows no viable mint growth.

plant_audit.p1_string_of_nickels: Stable, low-density foliage, consistent moisture levels.
plant_audit.p2_mexican_mint: Systemic loss; no vegetative material detected.
plant_audit.p3_pothos: Stressed; one large leaf shows significant chlorosis and necrosis (browning/yellowing).
plant_audit.p4_silver_guest: Active; single healthy leaf remains, showing slight turgor pressure.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently damp, dark substrate across all pots.
biome_observations.surface_debris: White perlite/grit visible; eggshell fragments present in P4/P2 pot.

temporal_deltas.t_minus_5_to_current: The P3 Pothos leaf has transitioned from a healthy green to a necrotic yellow-brown state. The P4 Silver Guest leaf has remained relatively static in posture, indicating a potential growth stall.

vision_report.anomalies: The P3 Pothos leaf is undergoing rapid senescence. The P2 Mexican Mint is entirely absent, suggesting a failure to establish or total decay.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of decline. The P3 Pothos is exhibiting clear signs of physiological stress, likely due to root-zone issues or light-spectrum inadequacy. The P2 Mexican Mint has been lost entirely, leaving the P4 Silver Guest as the sole occupant of the secondary black pot. The environment remains stable in terms of moisture, but the biological health of the specimens is trending downward.
